methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of a field experiment to study the effects of using fresh and cryopreserved pollen on hybridization in horticultural plants. The experiment was conducted to evaluate the success of pollination and seed development under controlled conditions.

steps.description: The experiment was conducted under field condition in the block VII of Indian Institute of Horticultural Research, Hessaraghatta, Bangalore during August 2006 to June 2008.
steps.searchable_summary: set up field experiment at specified location and time

steps.description: The experiment consisted of four cross combinations, each of the cross combination was separately crossed with fresh and cryopreserved pollens.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare cross combinations with fresh and cryopreserved pollen

steps.description: The pollen of the genotypes: Kum Kum, Shobha, Hybrid selection 88-10-22', and Psittacinus hybrid were cryopreserved for a period of 150 days in liquid nitrogen.
steps.searchable_summary: cryopreserve pollen in liquid nitrogen for 150 days

steps.description: The first, second and third florets, which were showing color at bud stage were used for emasculation and the rest of florets from the fourth to the top were removed in order to enhance effective fertilization after being pollinated.
steps.searchable_summary: emasculate selected florets and remove others

steps.description: In morning three florets to be pollinated were emasculated by removing out all the anthers with forceps without injuring the pistil.
steps.searchable_summary: emasculate florets by removing anthers with forceps

steps.description: These emasculated florets were covered with perforated butter paper bags to prevent contamination by pollens from unknown sources.
steps.searchable_summary: cover emasculated florets with perforated bags

steps.description: Using fresh and cryopreserved pollen, pollination was effected in about 48 hours after emasculation.
steps.searchable_summary: pollinate florets with fresh and cryopreserved pollen

steps.description: Emasculated flowers were pollinated between 10.00 to 11.30 a.m. by gently rubbing dehisced anther against the sticky stigmatic surface.
steps.searchable_summary: perform pollination by rubbing anther on stigma

steps.description: Intervarietal and interspecific crosses were made during the last week of January.
After pollination, pollinated florets were covered with perforated butter paper bags and tied with soft thread.
Four different spikes of the same parent were used per replication for hybridization.
steps.searchable_summary: use four spikes per parent for hybridization

steps.description: There were a total of eight treatments tested in 2 x 4 factorial RCBD with five replications.
steps.searchable_summary: set up treatments in factorial RCBD

steps.description: Capsules were harvested in about one month after pollination.
Observations were recorded for number of well-developed seeds and underdeveloped seeds per capsule.
